text.title: Business Storytelling (Pyramid / SCQA / Pixar) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding storytelling. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Narrative MUST lead with the claim within the first 2 sentences. Burying the answer is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Audience stops reading the moment their question is answered; delay erodes retention.

rule.statement: Every fact MUST be paired with a 'so what' implication. Naked facts are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Facts without implications force the audience to infer the point themselves; the inference is often wrong.

rule.statement: Pixar narratives MUST use a strict causal 'because of that' chain: Once upon a time → every day → one day → because of that → because of that → until finally. Skipping links breaks reader cognition.
rule.rationale: Causal chain is what produces the emotional resonance; non-causal narratives feel like lists.

rule.statement: SCQA narratives MUST make the Complication explicit and surprising. A weak Complication produces no tension.
rule.rationale: Without tension the reader has no reason to keep reading; weak Complication kills the arc.

rule.statement: An artefact MUST use exactly one framework (Pyramid OR SCQA OR Pixar). Mixing is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mixed frameworks read as draft scaffolding the author forgot to remove.
